steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that on average, mammal studies showed larger differences than amphibian studies, but "for every class of animals examined, there were individual studies showing differences well above the average for mammals." That means some individual amphibian studies exceeded the mammal average. The answer must follow logically from this.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'differences more pronounced for amphibians than mammals': The passage says the average for mammals was higher, not lower. This contradicts the stated trend. ✗
  • Choice B — 'some amphibian studies found larger effects than some mammal studies': If individual amphibian studies exceeded the mammal average, they necessarily exceeded at least some mammal studies. This follows directly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Shannon's prairie dog study was larger than the average for prairie dog studies': The passage does not compare Shannon's study to the prairie dog average. This is not supported. ✗
  • Choice D — 'mammal studies more likely to specify whether effects were detrimental': The passage says nothing about whether studies specified the nature of effects. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
